footer.positioning: Secure-by-default project spaces for evidence readiness, documentation organization, customer-visible files, and private engineering docs.

hero.title: A structured shell for machine project records
hero.description: BuildTrace organizes the first product surface around evidence readiness, documentation organization, and a secure-by-default direction without connecting real customer or machine data.
hero.asideBody: The shell separates customer-visible files from private engineering docs while keeping real uploads, storage, QR flows, and machine records out of scope.

customerVisibleFiles.title: Clear future review area
customerVisibleFiles.body: The interface leaves room for files that customers may later review without adding document upload or download behavior.

privateEngineeringDocs.title: Separate internal space
privateEngineeringDocs.body: Private engineering material is positioned as a separate future area so internal work is not mixed with customer-facing records.

handoverReadiness.title: Organized preparation
handoverReadiness.body: The shell supports the idea of organized handover preparation without implying completion, approval, or regulatory outcomes.

landing.sectionsAriaLabel: Landing page evidence and documentation sections

evidence.heading: Evidence-readiness structure
evidence.body: Project records can be organized for review without promising legal, safety, or regulatory outcomes.

documentation.heading: Documentation boundaries
documentation.body: Customer-visible files and private engineering docs stay clearly separated so future project work can remain understandable.

privacy.heading: Privacy planning placeholders
privacy.body: The Phase 1 shell reserves space for privacy information without adding accounts, storage, or customer data workflows.

security.heading: Secure-by-default direction
security.body: The interface sets expectations for secure project organization while deeper security controls remain future implementation work.

dataProtection.heading: Data protection planning
dataProtection.body: Customer-visible files and private engineering docs are described as separate areas before any document handling features are added.

login.title: Secure sign-in entry shell
login.description: This route presents the future entry point without adding authentication, accounts, session handling, or password workflows.

emptyState.title: Authentication is not active yet
emptyState.body: The page shell is present so the translated product structure can be reviewed before real access flows are added.

preview.title: Prepared for a future protected workspace
preview.body: The visual shell sets expectations for a careful access experience while keeping authentication out of scope for this phase.

identity.title: Identity area placeholder
identity.body: A future sign-in flow can introduce account identity without changing this placeholder into a working form today.

session.title: Session boundary placeholder
session.body: The shell leaves room for future session behavior while no tokens, cookies, or access checks are added here.

boundary.title: Private workspace boundary
boundary.body: The entry point frames private engineering docs and customer-visible files as separate future workspace areas.

preview.note: No credentials are collected, validated, stored, or submitted from this placeholder.

dashboard.title: Dashboard page shell
dashboard.description: This route reserves space for a future project overview without connecting to data, storage, or live metrics.

emptyState.title: No dashboard data is connected
emptyState.body: The shell can frame future summaries for evidence readiness and documentation organization when implementation reaches that stage.

handoverReadiness.title: Future handover view
handoverReadiness.body: A later dashboard can organize project handover preparation without implying completion, approval, or review outcomes.

machineRecords.title: Machine context placeholder
machineRecords.body: The shell reserves a place for machine project records while no machine data, QR flow, or asset CRUD exists.

documentOrganization.title: Document structure placeholder
documentOrganization.body: Future summaries can separate customer-visible files from private engineering docs without adding uploads or storage here.

ticketActivity.title: Communication placeholder
ticketActivity.body: The dashboard can later reflect support or feedback activity while no ticket backend or operational workflow is active.

machines.title: Machines page shell
machines.description: This route reserves the future machine list without adding records, QR workflows, or asset CRUD.

emptyState.title: No machine records are available
emptyState.body: The placeholder keeps the navigation visible while machine project records remain future implementation work.

machineDetail.title: Machine project shell
machineDetail.description: This route shows the future machine detail structure without connecting real machine data, customer records, QR flows, documents, tickets, or spare-parts logic.

placeholder.title: Placeholder-only machine view
placeholder.body: The route is available for shell review, but no database, API, storage, document download, QR portal, or machine CRUD is connected.

overview.title: Overview placeholder
overview.body: A future view can summarize machine context while this shell does not load real identifiers, customer details, or technical records.

handoverReadiness.title: Readiness placeholder
handoverReadiness.body: This area can later organize handover preparation without claiming completion, approval, or review outcomes.

documents.title: Document placeholder
documents.body: Customer-visible files and private engineering docs can be separated later while no upload, storage, or download behavior exists here.

tickets.title: Ticket placeholder
tickets.body: Future communication can be framed here while no ticket backend, status logic, assignment, or operational workflow is active.

softwareTimeline.title: Timeline placeholder
softwareTimeline.body: A later product stage can organize software notes or release context while no real timeline data is stored or displayed.

spareParts.title: Parts placeholder
spareParts.body: Future spare-parts context can live here without inventory, ordering, database records, or asset CRUD.

documents.title: Documents page shell
documents.description: This route reserves space for customer-visible files and private engineering docs without adding upload or storage behavior.

emptyState.title: No document handling is active
emptyState.body: The shell shows where documentation organization can live later while file workflows remain out of scope.

tickets.title: Tickets page shell
tickets.description: This route reserves a future communication area without adding ticket creation, assignment, or status logic.

emptyState.title: No ticket workflow is active
emptyState.body: The placeholder keeps the product map complete without storing requests or creating operational records.

spareParts.title: Spare parts page shell
spareParts.description: This route reserves a future spare-parts area without adding inventory, ordering, or database behavior.

emptyState.title: No spare-parts data is connected
emptyState.body: The page shell is only a translated structure for later product planning.

feedback.title: Feedback page shell
feedback.description: This route reserves a future feedback area without collecting messages, files, or customer data.

emptyState.title: Feedback collection is not active
emptyState.body: The placeholder supports navigation review while feedback workflows remain future implementation work.

settings.title: Settings page shell
settings.description: This route reserves a future settings area without adding accounts, permissions, organization controls, or backend calls.

emptyState.title: No settings are configurable
emptyState.body: The shell marks where product preferences can live later without changing application behavior now.

userRole.title: Role planning placeholder
userRole.body: Future role visibility can help distinguish internal work from customer-visible areas without adding permissions today.

preferredLanguage.title: Language preference placeholder
preferredLanguage.body: The translated shell is ready for future preference handling while this page does not save language settings.

futureMfa.title: Multi-factor planning placeholder
futureMfa.body: The interface leaves room for future stronger access controls without activating authentication or MFA now.

dataExport.title: Export planning placeholder
dataExport.body: Future export concepts can be shaped around documentation organization without adding file generation or storage behavior.

securityLogs.title: Audit trail planning placeholder
securityLogs.body: A later product stage can introduce security event visibility while no logs are collected or displayed here.
